creator: odip@bionet.nsc.ru
text:
> Sounds subjective to me.  Might be a good topic for the Ports 
 > Team to come up with a documented procedure so everyone knows.
 
 Yes - in porters handbook !
 
 > >> 2) Did you also notice we have the same problem with pam_authsrv and
 > >> pam_pseudo?
 > >
 > > No, I can't see ...
 > 
 > ???
 > 
 > pirzyk@amigo:/usr/ports
 > 3>find . -name pkg-plist -print | xargs grep authtest
 > ./net/globus4/pkg-plist:%%GLOBUS_BASE%%/test/globus_authz_test/%% 
 > BASE_FLAVOR%%/shared/authtest
 > ./net/globus4/pkg-plist:%%GLOBUS_BASE%%/test/globus_authz_test
 > /authtest
 > ./security/pam_authsrv/pkg-plist:sbin/authtest
 > ./security/pam_per_user/pkg-plist:sbin/authtest
 > ./security/pam_pseudo/pkg-plist:sbin/authtest
 
 O!
 
 > 
 > The net/globus4 ones are not a problem.
 
 May be scan all pkg-plist* in all ports for conflicting names ?
 
 But exclude situation when conflicting names found in conflicted ports,
 like in net/samba3:
 
 odip@monk$ grep CONFLICT Makefile
 CONFLICTS?=             ja-samba-2.* ja-samba-3.* samba-2.* sharity-light-1.*
